project.name: Kuberna Labs
project.description: Kuberna Labs is a hybrid educational and technological platform that empowers developers to build, deploy, and manage autonomous AI agents operating across decentralized networks. It combines a hands-on learning environment with production-grade infrastructure for cross-chain finance, zero‑knowledge privacy, and trusted execution environments.
project.vision: To become the definitive operating system for the agentic economy, where intelligent agents seamlessly interact with both Web2 and Web3 systems, governed by cryptographic guarantees and decentralized infrastructure.
project.mission: Equip the next generation of founders and enterprises with the tools, knowledge, and secure infrastructure to build revenue‑generating autonomous agents that operate at machine speed across any blockchain.

course_management.description: Admins can create, update, and publish courses with modules, quizzes, and coding labs.

details.course_creation: Admins can input title, description, level, duration, price, thumbnail, learning objectives, prerequisites.
details.module_management: Each course can have multiple modules; modules can be reordered, edited, deleted.
details.bulk_upload: Admins can upload multiple modules via CSV or JSON.

workshop_scheduling.description: Live workshops with interactive coding environments and video streaming.

details.scheduling: Instructors can set date, time, duration, max participants.
details.streaming: Integrated with HLS low‑latency streaming; automatic recording.
details.interactive_code: Ephemeral TEE‑based coding environments for each participant.
details.chat: Real‑time chat with moderation; Q&A sessions.
details.reminders: Email and push notifications 24h and 15min before.
details.recording_availability: Recordings available for 30 days after workshop.

progress_tracking.description: Users can track completion, earn certificates, and view learning analytics.

details.progress_calculation: Percentage of completed modules; weighted by duration.
details.certificates: Generated as verifiable credentials on‑chain (Ethereum, NEAR). Includes QR code for verification.
details.analytics: Time spent per module, quiz scores, lab attempts, heatmaps of engagement.
details.export_progress: Users can export their progress as PDF.

sdk_delivery.description: Self‑serve SDK with templated agent code and documentation.

details.download: Via npm, pip, or direct GitHub release.
details.cli_tool: Command-line interface for project scaffolding and deployment.

forum.description: Community forum for each course and general discussion.

details.threads: Users can create threads, reply, upvote.
details.moderation: Instructors and admins can pin, lock, delete.

agent_builder_ide.description: Browser‑based IDE to write, test, and debug agents using ElizaOS and other frameworks.

details.editor: Monaco editor with syntax highlighting, autocomplete, linting.
details.terminal: Access to container shell for debugging.
details.run_button: Execute agent in sandbox; logs streamed.
details.templates: Quick start templates for common agent types.
details.ai_assistant: Built-in AI code completion and explanation (powered by fine‑tuned model).

version_control.description: Integration with GitHub for agent code versioning.

details.actions: Commit, push, pull, create PR directly from IDE.
details.sync: Automatic sync on save; conflict resolution.

template_library.description: Pre‑built agent templates for trading, governance, monitoring, etc.

each_template.example_usage: Code snippet showing how to customize.

testing_sandbox.description: Isolated environment to test agents with simulated blockchain.

details.assertions: Verify agent outputs and state changes.

intent_posting.description: Users can post tasks in natural language with a budget, which are converted to structured intents.

details.nlp_parsing: Using fine‑tuned LLM to extract intent parameters: source/destination chains, tokens, amount, deadline.
details.validation: Check if user has sufficient balance; estimate gas.
details.budget: User specifies max fee in NEAR or stablecoin; funds are locked in escrow.
details.draft_save: Users can save intents as drafts.

solver_network.description: Agents can discover and bid on intents; solvers compete to fulfill them.

details.discovery: NATS broadcast of new intents matching agent capabilities.
details.bidding: Agents submit bids with price and estimated completion time.
details.selection: Requester can manually accept or set auto‑accept rules (lowest price, fastest, highest reputation).
details.bid_retraction: Agents can retract bids before acceptance.

escrow_management.description: Funds locked in smart contract escrow, released upon verified completion.

details.escrow_contract: Deployed on multiple chains; supports ERC‑20 and native tokens.
details.release_conditions: Agent submits proof of completion; requester can auto‑release or dispute.
details.timeout: If requester doesn't act within 24h, funds auto‑released to agent (if proof valid).
details.refund: If task fails or is disputed in favor of requester, funds returned.
details.multi_chain: Escrow can handle cross‑chain via bridges.

reputation_system.description: Agents and solvers accumulate reputation based on success rate, speed, and feedback.

details.decay: Reputation decays over time if inactive.
details.badges: Special badges for top performers, e.g., 'Elite Solver'.

details.arbitration_panel: Uses Kleros or a trusted oracle.
details.evidence: Both parties can submit evidence (text, images, proofs).
details.appeal: Option to appeal with additional fee.

multi_chain_support.description: Agents can interact with Ethereum, Solana, NEAR, and other major chains via adapters.

tee_deployment.description: Agents can be deployed inside Trusted Execution Environments (Intel TDX, AMD SEV) with remote attestation.

details.key_management: Keys generated inside enclave, never exposed.
details.cost: Billed per hour of enclave runtime.

zk_tls_integration.description: Agents can fetch verified Web2 data using Reclaim Protocol and zkPass.

details.flow: Agent initiates session → user authenticates → proof generated → agent verifies on‑chain.
details.privacy: Only the required data is revealed, not credentials.

decompute.description: Agents can run on decentralized compute networks (Akash, Phala) to reduce costs.

details.bidding: Compute providers bid for agent workloads.
details.fallback: If no bids, fallback to centralized (AWS).
details.auto_scaling: Agents can scale based on demand.

logging_and_monitoring.description: Real‑time logs and metrics for deployed agents.

crypto_payments.description: Payments in NEAR, ETH, SOL, and stablecoins.

details.payment_flow: User approves token transfer to escrow contract.
details.gas_management: Users can pay gas in native token or have it deducted from payment.

fiat_onramp.description: Integration with MoonPay/Stripe for fiat‑to‑crypto conversions.

details.flow: User selects fiat amount → redirected → crypto sent to user's wallet.

subscription_billing.description: Recurring payments for enterprise tiers via crypto or credit card.

details.crypto_subscriptions: Via smart contract streaming (Superfluid) or manual renewal.
details.invoicing: Generate PDF invoices for enterprise clients.

withdrawals.description: Users can withdraw earnings to their wallet.

user_management.description: View and manage users, roles, permissions.

details.list: Paginated table with search and filters (role, status, date).
details.actions: Edit user, disable, delete, change role.
details.audit: View audit log for each user.
details.impersonate: Admin can impersonate user for support (with audit).

content_moderation.description: Review courses, workshops, and marketplace listings.

analytics_dashboard.description: Real‑time metrics on revenue, active agents, transaction volume.

system_health.description: Monitor service health, database, blockchain nodes.

feature_flags.description: Toggle features on/off for A/B testing or maintenance.

performance.page_load: First Contentful Paint < 1.5s, Time to Interactive < 3s

non_functional.scalability: Support 10,000 concurrent users, 1,000 active agents, and 100,000 transactions per day. Horizontal scaling via Kubernetes.
non_functional.availability: 99.9% uptime for critical services (gateway, marketplace, execution).
non_functional.security: All sensitive data encrypted at rest and in transit; smart contracts audited; TEEs for proprietary logic.
non_functional.compliance: GDPR compliant; SOC2 Type II for enterprise tier.
non_functional.usability: Mobile‑responsive design; onboarding < 5 minutes for basic SDK users. Follows iOS 26 design principles.
non_functional.maintainability: Modular microservices with clear APIs; comprehensive documentation.
non_functional.localization: Support for English, French, Spanish, Swahili initially.

user_stories.story: As a developer, I want to browse available courses and enroll in the 'Agentic Commerce Accelerator' so that I can learn how to build revenue‑generating AI agents.
acceptance_criteria: Course catalog displays title, description, price, and prerequisites.
Enrollment requires payment (crypto or fiat).
Upon payment, access to all modules and live workshops is granted.
I receive a welcome email with next steps.

user_stories.story: As a learner, I want to use the browser‑based IDE to build my agent following the course material, so that I can test my code without local setup.
acceptance_criteria: IDE loads my assigned starter code.
I can run the agent in a sandboxed environment.
Output logs are displayed in real time.
I can save my progress and resume later.
The IDE supports syntax highlighting for TypeScript, Python, Rust.

user_stories.story: As a learner, I want to receive a certificate upon course completion, so that I can showcase my skills.
acceptance_criteria: Certificate is generated as a verifiable credential on‑chain.
Certificate includes my name, course title, and completion date.
I can share it on LinkedIn or download as PDF.
Certificate has a unique verification URL.

user_stories.story: As a protocol founder, I want to post a task for an agent to monitor my protocol’s TVL and alert me on Discord if it drops by more than 10%, so that I can react quickly.
acceptance_criteria: I can create a task with natural language description and attach a budget.
The system converts it to an intent and broadcasts to the solver network.
I receive notifications when an agent accepts the task and when it completes.
I can see the agent's reputation before accepting.

user_stories.story: As a requester, I want to fund an escrow with NEAR tokens when posting a task, so that funds are only released upon successful completion.
acceptance_criteria: I approve a transaction to transfer tokens to the escrow contract.
The task status shows 'funded'.
If the task fails, I can reclaim my funds after a timeout.

user_stories.story: As an agent, I want to scan the marketplace for tasks that match my capabilities, so that I can bid on them and earn revenue.
acceptance_criteria: Agent receives a stream of open intents via NATS.
It evaluates each intent against its own skills and pricing.
It submits a bid with proposed price and estimated completion time.
Bids are stored and visible to requester.

user_stories.story: As an agent, I want to execute a cross‑chain swap task by generating an intent and letting solvers compete, so that I can get the best price without managing liquidity.
acceptance_criteria: Agent creates an intent with source/destination chains and tokens.
It receives quotes from solvers within the timeout.
It selects the best quote and confirms execution.
Upon completion, it submits a proof and receives payment.

user_stories.story: As an enterprise user, I want to deploy my trading agent inside a TEE with remote attestation, so that I can prove to my stakeholders that the trading logic is tamper‑proof.
acceptance_criteria: I can select 'TEE deployment' in the agent configuration.
The agent is packaged and deployed to a Phala enclave.
I receive a cryptographic attestation that can be verified on‑chain.
A dashboard shows the attestation status and enclave health.

user_stories.story: As an admin, I want to view real‑time metrics on platform usage, revenue, and agent activity, so that I can make data‑driven decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows graphs for new users, active agents, transaction volume, and revenue.
I can filter by date range and export reports.
Alerts are shown for anomalies (e.g., sudden drop in success rate).

user_stories.story: As a solver, I want to subscribe to intents and submit quotes, so that I can compete to fulfill tasks and earn fees.
acceptance_criteria: I receive intents matching my configured chains and capabilities.
I can submit quotes with price, time, and route details.
If selected, I execute the path and submit a proof.
I receive payment automatically from escrow.

user_stories.story: As a learner, I want to attend a live workshop where I can code alongside an instructor and ask questions, so that I can resolve blockers in real time.
acceptance_criteria: Workshop page shows upcoming sessions with registration.
During session, video stream and interactive coding environment are synced.
Chat allows questions and answers.
Recording is available afterward.

user_stories.story: As a requester, I want to view the reputation and past performance of agents before accepting a bid, so that I can choose a reliable worker.
acceptance_criteria: Each agent has a profile with success rate, average response time, and number of tasks completed.
I can see reviews from previous requesters.
I can filter bids by minimum reputation.

user_stories.story: As a learner, I want to download the SDK and run agents locally, so that I can develop without an internet connection.
acceptance_criteria: SDK available via npm/pip with documentation.
Local CLI tool for creating, testing, and deploying agents.
Offline mode with mock blockchain.

user_stories.story: As an enterprise user, I want to integrate zkTLS to fetch private data from my bank, so that my agent can prove my solvency on‑chain without revealing my balance.
acceptance_criteria: Agent can initiate a zkTLS session with my bank.
I authenticate via the bank's website.
Proof is generated and stored.
I can verify the proof on‑chain.

user_stories.story: As an admin, I want to manually trigger a refund for a disputed task, so that I can resolve customer issues.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can view dispute details.
Admin can choose to refund requester or release payment to agent.
Action is logged and user notified.

user_stories.story: As an instructor, I want to create a new course with modules, quizzes, and labs, so that I can share my knowledge.
acceptance_criteria: Course creation form includes all necessary fields.
I can preview the course before publishing.
I can add co-instructors.
I can schedule live workshops for the course.

user_stories.story: As an enterprise admin, I want to generate a compliance report of all agent activities within my organization, so that I can present it to auditors.
acceptance_criteria: Report includes all tasks, payments, TEE attestations.
Report can be exported as PDF or CSV.
Data is anonymized for non‑critical fields.
Report can be scheduled (daily, weekly, monthly).

user_stories.story: As a solver, I want to set my availability and preferred chains, so that I only receive intents I can fulfill.
acceptance_criteria: I can update my profile with supported chains and tokens.
I can set maximum concurrent tasks.
I can pause bidding when busy.

user_stories.story: As a learner, I want to ask questions in a dedicated forum for each course, so that I can get help from peers and instructors.
acceptance_criteria: Each course has a discussion forum.
I can post questions and replies.
Instructors can mark answers as correct.
I can upvote helpful answers.

user_stories.story: As a developer, I want to use the API to programmatically create agents and post intents, so that I can integrate Kuberna into my own platform.
acceptance_criteria: API documentation includes authentication and examples.
API keys can be generated in settings.
Rate limits are clearly documented.
Webhooks for task completion events.

user_stories.story: As a learner, I want to track my progress across multiple courses, so that I can see my overall learning journey.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows overall progress percentage.
List of completed and in‑progress courses.
Estimated time to completion for each.

user_stories.story: As a requester, I want to set auto‑accept rules for bids, so that I don't have to manually approve every bid.
acceptance_criteria: Rules based on price, reputation, estimated time.
Rule can be applied to specific intent or all future intents.
I can pause auto‑accept at any time.

user_stories.story: As an agent owner, I want to set a pricing model for my agent (fixed fee, hourly, success‑based), so that I can monetize appropriately.
acceptance_criteria: Agent configuration includes pricing options.
Pricing displayed to requesters.
Earnings tracked per task.

user_stories.story: As a user, I want to connect my wallet and see my balance across multiple chains, so that I can fund tasks easily.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows balances for NEAR, ETH, SOL, USDC.
One‑click funding for intents.
Transaction history displayed.

user_stories.story: As an enterprise user, I want to whitelist IP addresses that can access my agents, so that I can ensure only authorized systems interact.
acceptance_criteria: IP whitelist configuration in settings.
Agents reject requests from non‑whitelisted IPs.
Audit log of access attempts.

edge_cases.name: payment_failure
edge_cases.description: User's crypto transaction fails or takes too long.
edge_cases.handling: The system monitors transaction status; after timeout, it cancels the escrow and notifies the user. User can retry with different gas settings.

edge_cases.name: agent_misbehavior
edge_cases.description: Agent delivers incorrect or fraudulent result.
edge_cases.handling: Requester can dispute within 24 hours; dispute goes to a decentralized arbitration panel (Kleros) or a trusted oracle. If agent is at fault, funds are returned to requester and agent's reputation is penalized.

edge_cases.name: solver_timeout
edge_cases.description: Solver fails to complete intent within promised time.
edge_cases.handling: Escrow automatically expires; requester can reclaim funds. Solver's reputation decreases; repeated offenses lead to ban.

edge_cases.name: database_outage
edge_cases.description: Primary PostgreSQL database becomes unavailable.
edge_cases.handling: Read replicas serve read‑only traffic; writes are queued. Automatic failover to standby within 60 seconds.

edge_cases.name: tee_attestation_failure
edge_cases.description: TEE enclave fails attestation or is compromised.
edge_cases.handling: Deployment is halted; user is alerted. The system attempts to redeploy on another provider. All keys are rotated.

edge_cases.name: zk_tls_proof_invalid
edge_cases.description: Proof generated by agent does not verify.
edge_cases.handling: Task marked as failed; agent does not get paid. The proof is stored for audit. Requester can appeal.

edge_cases.name: cross_chain_bridge_delay
edge_cases.description: Bridge transaction takes longer than expected.
edge_cases.handling: Solver can extend deadline with requester approval; otherwise escrow times out and solver pays penalty.

edge_cases.name: user_registration_conflict
edge_cases.description: User tries to register with an email that already exists.
edge_cases.handling: Show error: 'Email already registered. Please log in or use a different email.' Provide password reset option.

edge_cases.name: course_payment_failure
edge_cases.description: Crypto payment for course fails due to insufficient funds.
edge_cases.handling: Course enrollment remains pending; user is notified to fund wallet. After 24 hours, the enrollment is cancelled.

edge_cases.name: agent_code_error
edge_cases.description: Agent code contains syntax error and fails to deploy.
edge_cases.handling: IDE highlights error line; deployment button disabled until fix. Error logs are shown to user.

edge_cases.name: intent_expired_no_bids
edge_cases.description: Intent posted but no bids received before deadline.
edge_cases.handling: Intent marked as expired; user notified. Funds are automatically refunded to requester's wallet.

edge_cases.name: duplicate_bid
edge_cases.description: Same agent submits multiple bids for the same intent.
edge_cases.handling: Only the latest bid is kept; previous ones are discarded.

edge_cases.name: requester_insufficient_balance
edge_cases.description: User tries to post intent but has insufficient balance.
edge_cases.handling: Error message with link to fund wallet. Option to use fiat on‑ramp.

edge_cases.name: agent_deployment_failure
edge_cases.description: Agent fails to deploy on chosen provider (e.g., Akash no bids).
edge_cases.handling: Fallback to another provider or notify user to retry later.

edge_cases.name: concurrent_bid_acceptance
edge_cases.description: Two requesters try to accept the same bid simultaneously.
edge_cases.handling: Use database locks; only first succeeds, second gets error.

edge_cases.name: certificate_generation_failure
edge_cases.description: On‑chain certificate minting fails due to gas issues.
edge_cases.handling: Retry with exponential backoff; if still fails, store off‑chain and notify admin.

edge_cases.name: rate_limit_exceeded
edge_cases.description: User exceeds API rate limit.
edge_cases.handling: Return 429 with Retry-After header. User notified via toast.

edge_cases.name: concurrent_agent_edit
edge_cases.description: Two users try to edit the same agent simultaneously.
edge_cases.handling: Implement optimistic locking; second user gets conflict error with option to reload.

edge_cases.name: dispute_no_response
edge_cases.description: Dispute raised but no resolution after 7 days.
edge_cases.handling: Auto‑resolve in favor of agent if proof valid, else refund requester.

edge_cases.name: workshop_overcapacity
edge_cases.description: Workshop reaches max participants while user tries to register.
edge_cases.handling: Show 'Workshop full' and option to join waitlist.

edge_cases.name: workshop_stream_down
edge_cases.description: Live stream fails during workshop.
edge_cases.handling: Fallback to backup stream; notify participants via chat.

edge_cases.name: agent_unauthorized_access
edge_cases.description: Agent tries to access resource without proper API key.
edge_cases.handling: Return 401; log attempt for audit.

edge_cases.name: stale_agent
edge_cases.description: Agent has not pinged for more than 1 hour.
edge_cases.handling: Mark as inactive; stop sending tasks.

edge_cases.name: webhook_delivery_failure
edge_cases.description: Webhook to external system fails.
edge_cases.handling: Retry with backoff up to 5 times; if still fails, log and alert admin.

edge_cases.name: gas_price_spike
edge_cases.description: Gas price spikes during escrow funding.
edge_cases.handling: User warned; option to wait or increase budget.

edge_cases.name: forum_spam
edge_cases.description: User posts spam in forum.
edge_cases.handling: Auto‑detect with ML; flag for moderation; if repeated, ban user.

visual_design.headline: Architect the Agentic Web3 Enterprise
visual_design.subheadline: Build, deploy, and monetize autonomous AI agents on decentralized infrastructure.

components.responsiveness: Stack on mobile, model below text

backend_services.name: api-gateway
backend_services.description: GraphQL gateway that aggregates all microservices, handles authentication and rate limiting.
backend_services.rate_limiting: 100 req/min per IP, 1000 req/min per authenticated user

backend_services.name: user-service
backend_services.description: Manages user accounts, authentication, profiles, roles.

backend_services.name: course-service
backend_services.description: Handles courses, modules, enrollments, progress, certificates.

backend_services.name: agent-service
backend_services.description: Manages agent definitions, configurations, versioning, and lifecycle.

backend_services.name: marketplace-service
backend_services.description: Handles intents, bidding, task assignment, reputation.

backend_services.name: execution-service
backend_services.description: Orchestrates agent execution on various compute providers (Akash, Phala). Communicates with agents via NATS.

backend_services.name: payment-service
backend_services.description: Handles crypto payments, escrow interactions, fiat on‑ramp.

backend_services.name: tee-service
backend_services.description: Manages TEE deployments, attestation verification, and secure key management.

backend_services.name: blockchain-listener
backend_services.description: Listens to on‑chain events (escrow, payments) and updates database accordingly.

backend_services.name: worker
backend_services.description: Background job processor for email notifications, cleanup tasks, scheduled reports.

backend_services.name: analytics-service
backend_services.description: Aggregates metrics and serves analytics data for dashboards.

backend_services.name: dispute-service
backend_services.description: Handles dispute resolution, integrates with arbitration panel.

backend_services.name: reporting-service
backend_services.description: Generates compliance and analytics reports for enterprise users.

backend_services.name: notification-service
backend_services.description: Manages in‑app notifications, email, Discord, Telegram.

background_jobs.name: intent-expiration
background_jobs.description: Mark intents as expired if they have passed their deadline and no bid was accepted. Refund escrow if funded.

background_jobs.name: task-timeout
background_jobs.description: Check tasks that have been executing longer than the estimated time; if overdue, mark as failed and notify requester.

background_jobs.name: attestation-renewal
background_jobs.description: Renew TEE attestations that are about to expire; redeploy if necessary.

background_jobs.name: send-notification-emails
background_jobs.description: Process notification queue and send emails via Resend/SES.

background_jobs.name: aggregate-analytics
background_jobs.description: Compute daily metrics and store in ClickHouse for reporting.

background_jobs.name: cleanup-old-logs
background_jobs.description: Delete audit logs older than 90 days and archive to cold storage.

background_jobs.name: sync-onchain-events
background_jobs.description: Poll blockchain for missed events (fallback for listener).

background_jobs.name: reputation-calculation
background_jobs.description: Recalculate reputation scores for agents based on task history.

background_jobs.name: course-certificate-issuance
background_jobs.description: Check for completed courses and issue certificates on‑chain.

background_jobs.name: agent-health-check
background_jobs.description: Ping deployed agents; mark as inactive if no response.

background_jobs.name: payment-settlement
background_jobs.description: Settle pending payments (e.g., release escrow after task completion and no dispute).

background_jobs.name: workshop-reminder
background_jobs.description: Send reminders for upcoming workshops (24h and 15min before).

background_jobs.name: dispute-auto-resolve
background_jobs.description: Automatically resolve disputes older than 7 days.

background_jobs.name: stale-intent-cleanup
background_jobs.description: Delete draft intents older than 30 days.

background_jobs.name: newsletter-digest
background_jobs.description: Send weekly newsletter to subscribers with platform updates.

backend.caching: Redis 7 for session data, rate limits, and temporary storage
backend.distributed_locks: Redlock for critical sections (e.g., bid acceptance)

error_handling.strategy: All errors are logged, categorized, and returned in a consistent format.

image resource limits; execution logs streamed; use WebSocket for real‑time logs.

to handle spikes; separate queues for different priorities.

update_mechanism.frontend: Next.js automatic static regeneration; new deploymentsNext.js automatic static regeneration; are seamless. Users get new version on next page load.
update_mechanism.backend_services: Rolling updates in Kubernetes; canary deployments for critical services with 10% traffic new deployments are seamless. Users get new version on next page load.
Rolling updates in Kubernetes; canary deployments for critical services with 10 shift, then 100% if success.

Upgradable via proxy pattern (UUPS) for escrow; governance for core contracts (multi‑sigsig).
update_mechanism.agents: Agents can be updated by redeploying with new code; versioning supported. Old version remains until new one is healthy.
update_mechanism.database_schema: Migrations run automatically before new version starts; backward‑compatible changes only.
